title: Container Delivery, Health, Migration Coordination, and Safe Rollout · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed operations review reconstructs container delivery, health, migration coordination, and safe rollout from revision, tenant, source URL, request validators, response bytes, parsed identity, transaction, lease, state transition, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for deployable Go feed-service release unit after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Build a minimal non-root pinned multi-stage image with exact binary, migrations, certificates, ownership, metadata, SBOM, signature, and vulnerability evidence.
Validate configuration, secrets references, database compatibility, migration state, network policy, budgets, and dependency reachability before serving.
Separate liveness, readiness, startup, degraded ingestion, backlog, database, and maintenance states without exposing secrets or causing restart loops.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A review Go team handles case 1591 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to bind non-root artifacts, startup, health, migration ownership, compatibility, canary, and rollback evidence; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, date, identity, content, or encoding case and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Build a minimal non-root pinned multi-stage image with exact binary, migrations, certificates, ownership, metadata, SBOM, signature, and vulnerability evidence.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Feed Go Progressive Rollout, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, import one convenient trusted RSS feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, XML and HTTP budgets, PostgreSQL constraints and transaction, schedule, lease, goroutine cleanup, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as feed-service proof.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Progressive Rollout
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Feed Go Progressive Rollout: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go team handles case 7785 during an isolated backup restore exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to bind non-root artifacts, startup, health, migration ownership, compatibility, canary, and rollback evidence; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Canary compatible binaries and schema with traffic, worker, freshness, error-budget, rollback, and kill-switch evidence.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Progressive Rollout.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Progressive Rollout, keep Feed Go Progressive Rollout connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Canary compatible binaries and schema with traffic, worker, freshness, error-budget, rollback, and kill-switch evidence.

hints: For Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Progressive Rollout, name the reader, publisher, revision, Go, source, tenant, URL, feed format, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, API, accessibility, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Feed Go Progressive Rollout precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Progressive Rollout, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review Go team handles case 7785 during an isolated backup restore exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to bind non-root artifacts, startup, health, migration ownership, compatibility, canary, and rollback evidence; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Canary compatible binaries and schema with traffic, worker, freshness, error-budget, rollback, and kill-switch evidence.

For Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Progressive Rollout, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A successful container launch justifies immediate full rollout.
